title: iPhone 17 Pro Max vs IQOO 15 Qual é o MELHOR? (Comparativo)
transcript: Which is better: iPhone 17 Pro Max or Vivo IQ 15 ?  To begin this comparison, we will start with the screen specifications of both cell phones. Regarding the screen, the iPhone 17 Pro Max comes with a 6.9-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display , Quad HD with a 120Hz refresh rate and 460 ppi.  In person, the IQO 15 comes with a 6.85-inch Quad HD Plus AMOLED screen with a 144Hz refresh rate and 508 PPI, relative to the phone's weight. The iPhone 17 Pro Max weighs 231g and is 8.75mm thick. The Vivo IQ 15 weighs 220g and is 8.17mm thick.  Regarding the cameras, the iPhone 17 Pro Max comes with a triple camera setup, with a 48-megapixel main camera and an additional 48- megapixel ultrawide camera.  And the other is a 48-megapixel telephoto camera.  The vivo IQ 15 comes with a triple camera setup, featuring a 50-megapixel main camera and an additional 50-megapixel ultrawide camera. And the other is a 50-megapixel telephoto camera.  As for the front camera, the iPhone 17 Pro Max comes with an 18-megapixel front camera. The Vivo IQ 15 comes with a 32-megapixel front camera. Regarding the battery, the iPhone 17 Pro Max comes with a 4832 mAh battery. The Vivo IQO 15 comes with a 7000 mAh battery.  In terms of performance, the iPhone 17 Pro Max features the A19 Pro processor, 4GB of RAM, and 512GB of internal storage.  In the vivo IQO 15, you'll find the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor with 12 GB of RAM and 512 GB of internal storage.  Well, after some research and testing, the Vivo IQO 15 came out on top in this regard.  But what about you, which one is the best?  iPhone 17 Pro Max or Vivo IQO 15?  Leave your comments below. title: A clever twist on MagSafe cases! Open Case for iPhone 17
transcript: If you've been around the channel for a while, you might remember my first video about Open Case when John was kind enough to send me an early working prototype. Back then, I was already impressed by how innovative the idea was, and now I get to try their official product officially this time. John sent me such a kind letter along with the package, and honestly, it means a lot to see how far they've come. It's always special when a small brand trusts you to help share their work with the world, especially when it's something that started from a simple brilliant idea. For anyone who didn't see that first video, Open Case is a patented iPhone case that completely rethinks how we use MagSafe accessories. Instead of letting them sit on top of the case like most do, Open Case has a unique open space that lets accessories sit inside the case. That simple idea fixes two of the biggest problems with Mags Safe thickness and movement. With most cases, when you attach a MagSafe wallet or battery, it adds bulk and it can easily shift or fall off when you slide it your phone when you slide your phone into your pocket or bag, for example. Open Case solves both by creating this open space that holds your accessories securely in place. It's one of those small but brilliant innovations that make you think that this is how Magsafe should have been from the start. So, this new official version feels even better than the prototype I tried months ago. It has the soft touch leatherrett finish that feels smooth, refined, and premium. It gives the case a leather-like texture without being bulky. It's lightweight, thin, and feels amazing in the hand. Definitely one of the coolest Mags Safe cases I've used. I also appreciate the sapphire camera control button because I really don't like cases that just leave the cutout there. This cover gives it a more polished and complete look and it feels great to press. It's one of those subtle details that instantly make a difference. And you also get all metal buttons and an anodized camera surround. The buttons respond really well. You know how picky I am about this and I can't stand cases where you have to press super hard just to get a click. This one's perfect. Nothing feels stiff or forced. It's smooth and precise, exactly how buttons should feel. Now, what makes Open Case [music] so special isn't just the design. It's how it feels in daily use. Because your accessory sits inside that open area, and your iPhone's overall profile becomes thinner and lighter. [music] So, it's more comfortable to hold. It fits easily into pockets and doesn't have that uneven bump that you usually get with your MagSafe accessories. [music] It also improves MagSafe charging performance. Apple recommends charging your iPhone with nothing between it and the charger. And since anything in between can affect performance with open case, that's exactly what you [music] get. Your phone charges directly on the back with nothing blocking the magnets. [music] And since accessories fit snugly into the open space, they stay perfectly aligned. [music] I can toss my iPhone in a bag, move around during the day, and everything stays securely in one place. Now, John also sent me three accessories that perfectly match [music] the case. The Mag Wallet, the filler plate, and the pop holder, all by open case. The Mag Wallet is sleek and minimal. It holds up to three cards and fits perfectly inside the open area. Made with the same soft [music] touch finish as the case. It really looks like it belongs there. The filler plate is one of those small but thoughtful accessories because when you're not using any Macsafe [music] accessory, you can pop it in to keep the back protected and maintain that clean, complete look. [music] It even has a small cutout so it's easy to remove if you need. And then there's the pop holder, which is a Maxave compatible grip that pops out when you [music] need it and then you can leave it flat when you don't. The magnets [music] are strong and it sits flush inside the open space as well, so it never feels too bulky. [music] All three share the same material, color, and texture, and it feels cohesive and intentional, like part of a single ecosystem. Now, a quick note about compatibility. [music] John mentioned that Apple recently changed their Magsafe wallet, so unfortunately, it no longer fits perfectly within the open cases open space. [music] And I also tried another Maxafe accessory that I already own and it doesn't fit 100% [music] flush, but it definitely works and looks great. So, just keep in mind that compatibility can vary depending on the exact design and magnet layout of each accessory that you might have. But still, for most Macsafe accessories, especially the ones made by Open Case, everything fits beautifully and works exactly as intended. Open case is now available for the iPhone 17 lineup and comes in this sleek black finish with all metal buttons and the premium leather-like texture. [music] It's honestly amazing to see how far this brand has come from that early prototype I reviewed months ago and this polished official version. It's been such a thoughtful evolution and I also love the packaging. It looks so so chic. And I also really appreciate that open case is a small project built by John and his wife. My team here is also just me and my husband. So, I completely understand the amount of love and dedication that goes into something like this. I've always loved supporting small, passionate teams that create genuinely useful products, and Open Case is the perfect example of that. Smart design, quality materials, and a real focus on user experience. If you're looking for a MacSafe compatible case that's thinner, lighter, and feels truly premium, I can definitely recommend checking it out. >> [music] >> I've included a link for Open Cas's website in the description and make sure to use my code marina tech to get 10% off your first purchase. It's been such an honor to be part of your journey and to see Open Case grow into what it is today.

title: How to Remove Gmail from Mail App on iPhone 17 (Pro, Max) (2 Methods)
transcript: Here are two ways to remove a Gmail account from the Mail app. Either remove it completely or just hide it from the Mail app. First, open the Settings app on your iPhone. Scroll down to apps at the bottom and find the Mail app. Under mail accounts, choose the account you want to remove completely from the Mail app and stop syncing with mail, contacts, calendars, and notes. Tap Delete account, then confirm by tapping delete from my iPhone. your Gmail account will stay safe and you can still use it on other devices or on the web. So, no worries. To delete a Gmail account completely, check out my separate tutorial for that. If you only want to hide it from the device, just turn off all the toggles to hide it from the related apps. For example, if you want to hide it from the mail app, disable the mail toggle, then go back to the mail app. Under mailboxes, the Gmail account won't show up anymore, but you can still use contacts and other features linked to it on your iPhone. I hope you like this video. Don't forget to like, share, and subscribe for new free tech guide video updates.

title: How to Maintain 100% Battery Health iPhone 17 Pro Max | Pro [STOP DAMAGING]
transcript: Want to preserve the battery health of your iPhone 17, 17 Pro, or Pro Max and keep it at 100% as long as possible? Let's clear up some common myths you have probably seen online and show you what actually helps maintain the battery health of your iPhone. One of the most common misconceptions when it comes to battery health of your iPhone is charging while using it. But simply using your phone while it's on charge doesn't mean that you're damaging your battery and affecting the battery health. But what actually causes the battery health to go down is extreme temperature. The operating temperature of your iPhone's battery is 10° C to 22°. The ideal charging temperature of your iPhone is between 16 to 22°. This means if your iPhone's battery gets too hot or if you live in an environment that has sub-zero room temperature, then this will prevent your iPhone's battery from holding the maximum capacity of charge. So, you can use your phone while it's on charge or even game on it as long as the phone doesn't get too hot. And what brings down the battery health in the long run is the charging cycle count. So, on your iPhone 17, you can check the cycle count by opening settings, then going to battery, then scroll down, go to battery health, and here you'll see your cycle count. And according to Apple, the battery health may reach 80% when you complete 1,000 cycle counts. So, if your cycle count is higher, then the maximum capacity or battery health should naturally go down. So, this means if you want to preserve your iPhone's battery health, you'll need to lower the cycle count. And you can start by enabling battery optimization. Just go back to battery, open charging, and set the charge limit to 80%. The clean energy charging doesn't matter. And speaking of good charging habit, you should not charge your iPhone with any random charging adapters. So either stick to official Apple adapter and charging cable or get your charging accessories from reputed brands. Another thing that may lead to battery draining and eventually higher charging cycles is outdated or buggy iOS. So you should try to avoid using beta iOS and make sure it's always up to date. And another thing that you can do to reduce the charging cycles, which will improve the battery longevity of your iPhone is reducing your phone usage or make some changes to lower battery consumptions. For example, you can turn off always on display by going to the display and brightness settings and also turn off background app refresh by going to general and background app refresh. So, it means to preserve your iPhone's battery health, you just need to follow these simple rules. And that's all there is to it. Let us know your feedback and stay tuned for

title: 💪 Czy iPhone Air naprawdę ma taką samą wydajność jak iPhone 17 Pro? 💪
transcript: Does the iPhone R have the same performance as the iPhone Pro?  On the Apple website we have information that the iPhone R houses the power of the Pro and inside we will find the same A19 Pro chip.  So can we count on the same performance for both phones? The iPhone scored 1664 points in the 3D Mark test , and the iPhone 17 Pro scored 2029 points. So the iPhone R has about 18% less power. In the AnTuTu performance test, the iPhone R scored 13% worse than the iPhone 17 Pro.  In practice, Assassin's Creed Mirage stutters a bit on the highest graphics settings on both phones, while Fortnite on Epic runs great on both.  In everyday use, the iPhone is indeed slightly slower, but it is not particularly noticeable.
